project_reference.core_research_question: Can ZPE-Mocap be advanced from a boundary-confused staging repo to an evidence-backed commercialization wedge with honest final statuses for the mocap claims?
project_reference.current_focus: All roadmap phases complete; evidence ready for review

scope.question: Can ZPE-Mocap be advanced from a boundary-confused staging repo to an evidence-backed commercialization wedge with honest final statuses for the mocap claims?
in_scope: Treat /Users/Zer0pa/ZPE/ZPE Mocap/ZPE-Mocap as the sole GPD authority surface.
Carry forward GitHub issue #1 and Comet as evidence/log sinks rather than success proxies.
Use Mac M1 Air and Red Magic 10 Pro+ via ADB as the initial compute path.
Research and execute the unresolved gate surfaces already visible locally: clean boundary truth, Gate M2 live runtime evidence, and Gate F commercialization closure.
Manage disk tightly and delete only large outputs that are irrelevant to this mocap workstream.
out_of_scope: Promoting imported historical prose as current truth.
Counting the current 2/2 unit tests, synthetic-only metrics, or simulated Blender/USD checks as commercialization closure.
Escalating to RunPod without a documented IMP-COMPUTE basis and explicit user approval.
Editing other sector repositories or mutating the GitHub board directly.
unresolved_questions: Which exact closure-brief language needs to be restored beyond the locally recoverable PRD and runbook corpus?
Which imported historical artifacts remain usable anchors after stale IMC-era wording and path references are normalized?

user_asserted_anchors: Commercial wedge only counts if final claim statuses are PASS, FAIL, or PAUSED_EXTERNAL.
GitHub and Comet are evidence sinks, not success proxies.
RunPod requires explicit approval after a real IMP-COMPUTE blocker.
Keep docs and handover artifacts frozen unless the real gate is met.
known_good_baselines: Inner repo origin is https://github.com/Zer0pa/ZPE-Mocap on branch main.
Current minimal sanity surface is python -m unittest discover -s code/tests -v with 2/2 passing.
Fresh 2026-03-20 repo-local checkpoints report PASS for Gate A, Gate B, Gate C, Gate D, Gate M1, Gate M2, Gate M3, Gate M4, Gate E appendix, Gate E G5, Gate F, and Gate E.
Historical February 20, 2026 proof artifacts remain lineage only until revalidated from the inner repo boundary.
context_gaps: The exact closure brief text from the chat is not persisted on disk.
Imported historical artifacts still contain stale absolute paths and IMC-era wording.

formulations: Inner-repo-first execution contract tied to repo-facing truth docs and gate runbooks.
Popper-first gate closure with explicit PASS, FAIL, or PAUSED_EXTERNAL adjudication.
allowed_estimator_families: Direct gate evidence from repo-local runs, receipts, and claim adjudication artifacts.
Commercial-safe corpus and runtime evidence, or explicit IMP-backed PAUSED_EXTERNAL adjudication when execution is blocked.
forbidden_estimator_families: Simulated-only runtime proof presented as a live runtime pass.
Board status, GitHub issue presence, or Comet activity treated as proof of claim closure.
allowed_fit_families: Gate-by-gate closure aligned to the local M2 and F runbook requirements.
Conservative downgrade logic when decisive commercial-safe evidence is absent.
forbidden_fit_families: Narrative pass construction from mixed evidence.
Local improvements that substitute for the commercialization authority metric.
stop_and_rethink_conditions: Any regression on the commercialization authority metric.
Any final claim remains INCONCLUSIVE at closure time.
External compute or external datasets become necessary without documented IMP-* reasoning and user approval where required.

observables.name: Inner-repo authority boundary status
observables.definition: Whether execution, evidence, and docs are governed by the inner repo truth surface instead of outer workspace lineage.

observables.name: Gate M2 runtime evidence status
observables.definition: Whether Blender/USD runtime evidence is live, blocked with IMP-backed evidence, or still unresolved.

observables.name: Final mocap claim-status map
observables.definition: Status assignment over MOC-C001 through MOC-C007 at commercialization closure.

claims.statement: Execution decisions are governed by inner-repo truth and gate artifacts rather than outer-workspace lineage or proxy status surfaces.
Gate M2 ends with either repo-local live runtime evidence or an explicit IMP-backed downgrade that never promotes simulated-only checks as a live pass.
Commercialization closure assigns PASS, FAIL, or PAUSED_EXTERNAL to the final mocap claims with no INCONCLUSIVE residue.

deliverables.description: Project contract narrative that names the inner repo boundary, decisive gate surfaces, and forbidden proxy wins.
Roadmap that sequences boundary normalization, runtime evidence work, and commercialization adjudication without filler phases.
Runtime roundtrip evidence artifact with live-attempt annotations or IMP-backed blocked status.
Claim-to-resource closure map for the commercialization path.
must_contain: commercial-safe evidence path or IMP-backed fallback

deliverables.description: Final commercialization claim adjudication with PASS, FAIL, or PAUSED_EXTERNAL only.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-boundary
acceptance_tests.procedure: Review .gpd/PROJECT.md and .gpd/ROADMAP.md against the repo-facing truth docs and runbook to confirm the inner repo is named as authoritative and proxy wins are explicitly rejected.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The project scaffold explicitly binds execution to the inner repo boundary, preserves the gate sequence, and rejects synthetic-only, simulated-only, GitHub-only, and Comet-only closure logic.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-runtime
acceptance_tests.procedure: Inspect the runtime artifact and accompanying logs to verify that a live Blender/USD attempt was made from the repo boundary, or that an IMP-backed blocked outcome was recorded without simulated-only promotion.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The runtime artifact records either a live repo-local runtime attempt with evidence, or an explicit IMP-backed blocked state; simulated-only checks are not promoted as live proof.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-commercial
acceptance_tests.procedure: Compare commercialization_claim_adjudication.json and max_claim_resource_map.json against the Gate F rule set to confirm that every final claim status is PASS, FAIL, or PAUSED_EXTERNAL and that the status is backed by a commercial-safe evidence path or IMP-* downgrade.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No final claim remains INCONCLUSIVE, and every PASS or PAUSED_EXTERNAL status has an evidence-backed explanation consistent with the Gate F commercialization rules.

references.why_it_matters: Defines the lane claims, falsification plan, hard gates, and maximalization path that the closure work must respect.
States the current repo-facing truth surface and explicitly lists what is not yet promoted.
Defines the current audit limits and blocks over-promotion of historical or absent evidence.
Summarizes the current G1 cleanroom-boundary reading order and preserves the current truth interpretation.
Defines the canonical gate order, claim rules, fallback logic, and commercialization closure discipline.
Sets the runtime evidence bar and blocks simulated-only promotion.
Sets the final commercialization adjudication rules and forbids INCONCLUSIVE residue.
Tracks the lane orchestration context but is not itself evidence of gate closure.
Provides an execution log anchor for the boundary sanity run but does not close any gate by itself.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-boundary
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Treating outer-workspace narratives, GitHub issue state, or Comet activity as proof that the inner repo boundary is clean and authoritative.
forbidden_proxies.reason: Those surfaces can support traceability but they do not replace repo-local truth or gate evidence.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-runtime
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Presenting simulated Blender/USD checks or adapter smoke tests as a live runtime pass.
forbidden_proxies.reason: Gate M2 requires live runtime evidence or explicit blocked-state adjudication.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-commercial
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Closing commercialization claims using synthetic-only, non-commercial, or absent-corpus evidence without an explicit FAIL or PAUSED_EXTERNAL downgrade.
forbidden_proxies.reason: This would substitute proxy progress for the commercialization authority metric.

weakest_anchors: The exact closure-brief phrasing still depends on chat history rather than a repo-local source file.
Some imported February 2026 narrative artifacts still need future normalization before public-facing reuse.
unvalidated_assumptions: The locally recoverable PRD and runbooks capture enough of the missing closure brief to support future public-doc cleanup.
competing_explanations: Future public messaging may still need wording changes even though the current repo-local gate chain is closed.
Imported historical prose can still contradict fresh artifacts if reused without normalization.
disconfirming_observations: Local runtime attempts show that Gate M2 cannot be evidenced and cannot be downgraded cleanly with IMP-backed records.
Commercial-safe evidence is absent for one or more final claims and no honest FAIL or PAUSED_EXTERNAL adjudication path can be completed from the repo boundary.
Planning or execution starts depending on GitHub or Comet status instead of repo-local gate evidence.

position.current_phase_name: Conditional Compute Escalation Or Closure Packaging
position.last_activity_desc: Full local gate chain reached PASS through Gate F and Gate E without compute escalation

active_calculations: Repo-local `.venv` remains the verified execution environment for the inner repo.
Gate M2 runtime evidence passed from the repo boundary with a fresh checkpoint and USD runtime artifact.
Gate M1, Gate M3, Gate M4, Gate F, and Gate E all passed from the repo boundary with fresh checkpoints.
Compute escalation was not needed because the local closure path reached Gate E PASS.
intermediate_results: Gate A, Gate B, Gate C, Gate D, Gate M1, Gate M2, Gate M3, Gate M4, Gate E appendix, Gate E G5, Gate F, and Gate E all have fresh PASS checkpoints dated 2026-03-20.
`handoff_manifest.json` records PASS across the max gate set with `regression_exit_code=0`.
`commercialization_claim_adjudication.json` and `max_claim_resource_map.json` now close the current claim set without `INCONCLUSIVE` residue.

decisions.summary: Phase 0: Use recommended GPD defaults for this workstream to keep research, plan checking, and verification active without supervised pauses.
Phase 0: GitHub and Comet are traceability surfaces only, not acceptance proxies.
Phase 1: Start with a boundary-and-capability audit because the gate scripts already exist in `code/scripts`.
Phase 1: Normalize the repo-local environment before classifying deeper blockers.
Phase 1: Advance to Phase 2 because Gate A and Gate B both passed from the repo boundary.
Phase 2: Use the repo-local `.venv` and available runtime candidates instead of waiting for system-level parity before executing Gate M2.
Phase 3: Trust fresh repo-local gate artifacts over imported historical prose when adjudicating commercialization claims.
Phase 4: Skip compute escalation because Gate E G5 marked extra compute unnecessary and the local closure path reached PASS.
Added Phase 05: Refinement, performance, and evidence-hygiene augmentation
decisions.rationale: Extends current milestone with the next ratified augmentation lane

decisions.summary: Added Phase 06: Technical closure: real-corpus validation, Comet wiring, PyPI packaging, clean-clone verification, GitHub freshness
decisions.rationale: Extends current milestone with new research phase

blockers: Host disk headroom is still tight and should be watched before generating larger artifacts or environments.

session.stopped_at: All current gate surfaces passed; evidence is ready for orchestrator review
